themes.heading: Documenti e visti
themes.text: Obbligatorio il passaporto, con validitГ  residua di almeno 6 (sei) mesi, al momento dellвҖҷarrivo nel Paese, recante una pagina intera libera; obbligatorio il visto. Consultare attentamente la Sezione вҖңRequisiti di IngressoвҖқ di questa Scheda, per maggiori informazioni.

themes.heading: Vaccinazioni
themes.text: Febbre gialla, obbligatoria per i viaggiatori provenienti dai Paesi a rischio di trasmissione della malattia. Per informazioni sulle malattie presenti, consultare la Sezione вҖңSituazione SanitariaвҖқ di questa Scheda. Per ulteriori consigli, in merito a vaccinazioni raccomandate, tuttavia non obbligatorie, si raccomanda di consultare il proprio Medico / Centro Vaccinale di riferimento.

themes.heading: Aree di particolare cautela
themes.text: I viaggi nel Paese sono sconsigliati, se non dettati da ragioni di necessitГ , lavoro o affari. I viaggi per turismo sono sconsigliati, anche qualora parte di pacchetti organizzati da Agenzie di Viaggio/Tour Operators. Per maggiori informazioni, consultare attentamente la Sezione вҖңSicurezzaвҖқ di questa Scheda.

themes.heading: Ambasciata
themes.text: Ambasciata d'Italia a TRIPOLI Indirizzo: Shara Uahran, 1 P.O. Box 912 e-mail: Ambasciata.tripoli@esteri.it tel: +218 21 333.41.31 +218 21 333.41.32 +218 21 333.41.33 Cellulare di emergenza: +218 (0)91 5766666

themes.heading: Indicazioni generali, ordine pubblico e criminalita'
themes.text: L'Ambasciata dвҖҷItalia a Tripoli ha ripreso le proprie attivitГ , l'11 gennaio 2017; il Consolato Generale a Bengasi, competente per la Cirenaica e l'Est del Paese, ha riaperto il 28 giugno 2021. I viaggi nel Paese sono sconsigliati, se non dettati da ragioni di necessitГ , lavoro o affari. I viaggi per turismo sono sconsigliati, anche qualora parte di pacchetti organizzati da Agenzie di Viaggio/Tour Operators. Alle Imprese o Liberi Professionisti italiani, che operassero nel Paese, si ricorda di adottare ogni misura di sicurezza attiva e passiva, ritenuta idonea a tutelare il proprio personale, sulla base degli obblighi che discendono dalla vigente legislazione, in materia di sicurezza sui luoghi di lavoro. Il cessate il fuoco del 23 ottobre 2020 ha posto fine alle ostilitГ  e da quel momento non si sono verificate violazioni del cessate il fuoco tra il Governo di UnitГ  Nazionale e l'Esercito Nazionale Arabo Libico. La situazione di sicurezza nei centri cittadini di Tripoli, Misurata e Bengasi ГЁ in progressivo miglioramento nel 2026, ancorchГ© si registrino scontri armati episodici, prevalentemente in zone periferiche o in localitГ  costiere ad ovest della capitale, quale Zavia. Continuano a verificarsi episodi di detenzioni arbitrarie L'Aeroporto e le principali Compagnie Aeree continuano ad operare con regolaritГ , anche con voli per l'Italia.

themes.heading: Rischio terrorismo
themes.text: Rimane latente, in tutto il Paese, la minaccia terroristica, in particolare nel Fezzan e a sud di Sirte e possibile il rischio di rapimenti, specie al di fuori dei centri urbani di Tripoli, Misurata e Bengasi. La regione meridionale e le principali arterie stradali del Paese sono a rischio di criminalitГ  e la presenza di posti di blocco, talvolta controllati da gruppi armati irregolari. A seguito di attentati, negli anni 2015 - 2017, alcuni grandi alberghi della capitale si sono dotati di misure di sicurezza, in linea con gli standard di altri Paesi dell'area.

themes.heading: Aree di particolare cautela
themes.text: I viaggi nel Paese sono sconsigliati, se non dettati da ragioni di necessitГ , lavoro o affari. I viaggi per turismo sono sconsigliati, anche qualora parte di pacchetti organizzati da Agenzie di Viaggio/Tour Operators. Alle Imprese o Liberi Professionisti italiani, che operassero nel Paese, si ricorda di adottare ogni misura di sicurezza attiva e passiva, ritenuta idonea a tutelare il proprio personale, sulla base degli obblighi che discendono dalla vigente legislazione, in materia di sicurezza sui luoghi di lavoro.

themes.heading: Avvertenze
themes.text: Per chi decidesse di recarsi comunque nel Paese, sotto la propria responsabilitГ , si raccomanda, in particolare di: - registrare i dati del proprio viaggio su DOVESIAMONELMONDO, informare della propria presenza l'Ambasciata d'Italia tramite email all'indirizzo tripoli.connazionali@esteri.it, il Consolato Generale all'indirizzo consolato.bengasi@esteri.it in caso di viaggi nell'Est del Paese, e seguire attentamente le informative dellвҖҷUnitГ  di Crisi e dellвҖҷAmbasciata, anche attraverso lвҖҷaccount Twitter @ItalyInLibya; - assumere adeguate misure di sicurezza, rafforzate in caso di spostamenti al di fuori del centro delle cittГ  di Tripoli, Misurata e Bengasi. Si sconsigliano vivamente, dato lвҖҷelevatissimo rischio che comportano, gli spostamenti in autonomia sul territorio; - adottare sempre comportamenti ispirati alla massima cautela e attenzione, evitando gli assembramenti, specialmente durante i fine settimana e dopo la preghiera del venerdГ¬; - recare con sГ© copia dei propri documenti e titoli di viaggio, conservando gli originali in luoghi custoditi; - evitare di effettuare riprese video o scattare fotografie a qualsiasi sito di rilevanza politica (Ministeri, Ambasciate, etc..), nonchГ© militare (inclusi porti, aeroporti e check-points).

themes.heading: Normative locali rilevanti
themes.text: E' richiesto un Permesso di sicurezza, generalmente a pagamento e dal prezzo variabile, da parte delle AutoritГ  locali per i cittadini italiani che si rechino a Bengasi o in altre aree della Cirenaica. Ai fini del rilascio si consiglia di rivolgersi a uno sponsor locale. Da verificare con sponsor locali le condizioni per il passaggio terrestre da Tripoli e Bengasi per i cittadini non libici.

themes.heading: Strutture sanitarie
themes.text: Le strutture sanitarie pubbliche sono inadeguate. Nonostante il servizio sanitario libico sia universale, si consiglia di sottoscrivere una assicurazione privata, che preveda il rimpatrio sanitario, da attuare in caso di incidenti che necessitino il ricovero. Ogni qualvolta sia possibile, si consiglia pertanto il trasporto del paziente verso Italia, Tunisia o Malta. Malgrado la presenza di numerose farmacie, molti medicinali non sono reperibili. Chi necessitasse di farmaci specifici/particolari, deve portarli dallвҖҷItalia. Cliniche private - Alkhalil Hospital Az Zawiyah Street, Tripoli Tel. + 218 21 - 3612012 Referente amministrativo: + 218 91 9330463 - Libyan Swiss Clinic Tel. + 218 21 3619693 - Clinica Al Afia Gasser Ben Gashir Tel. + 218 21 5633051-4 / 021 5633900-4 Fax. + 218 21 5633055 - Clinica St. James Wesayat El Bderi, Ben Ashur + 218 91 335 8962 Tel + 218 21 3620242-3 Fax. + 218 21 3620241 e-mail: libya@stjameshospital.com

themes.heading: Malattie presenti
themes.text: Le AutoritГ  hanno sospeso tutte le misure restrittive, volte a contenere la diffusione del COVID-19. In concomitanza con l'accresciuto flusso migratorio, si registra un aumento di casi di malattie infettive.

themes.heading: Avvertenze
themes.text: Al pari di altri Paesi dell'area, si consiglia di bere solo acqua minerale in bottiglia, controllando che il tappo sia correttamente sigillato e di non aggiungere ghiaccio alle bevande. ГҲ sconsigliato mangiare verdura cruda e frutta non sbucciata e molluschi. Si segnala che si sono registrati casi di avvelenamento, a seguito del consumo di alcolici preparati in casa e/o distribuiti illegalmente. Nel rimarcare i rischi per la salute di tale pratica, si ricorda che in Libia vige il divieto assoluto di produzione e consumo di bevande alcoliche. Allo stesso modo, ГЁ proibita l'introduzione nel Paese di alcolici. Presso gli aeroporti e i varchi di frontiera, sono attivi scanner per la rilevazione di alcolici in ingresso e il portatore puГІ incorrere in gravi sanzioni, anche detentive, in caso venga individuato dalle locali AutoritГ .

themes.heading: Vaccinazioni
themes.text: Il certificato di vaccinazione contro la febbre gialla ГЁ obbligatorio, per i viaggiatori provenienti dai Paesi a rischio di trasmissione della malattia. Per altre vaccinazioni raccomandate tuttavia non obbligatorie, si invita a consultare il proprio Medico / Centro Vaccinale di riferimento.

themes.heading: Mobilita'
themes.text: Al momento, sono attivi collegamenti verso l'estero, dagli Aeroporti di Tripoli (Mitiga), di Misurata e di Bengasi. Esistono collegamenti diretti tra Italia (Roma Fiumicino, Milano Malpensa) e Libia (Tripoli, Bengasi) e collegamenti verso Malta, Tunisi, Istanbul, Gedda, Dubai, Amman, Dubai, Damasco, Il Cairo e Alessandria. Per informazioni di carattere generale sulla sicurezza dei voli e sulle Compagnie Aeree dei Paesi cui ГЁ vietato operare nello spazio aereo UE, in quanto non in regola con gli standard di sicurezza dell'Agenzia Europea per la Sicurezza Aerea, si consiglia di consultare la sezione Sicurezza aerea curata in collaborazione con l'Enac e il sito della Commissione Europea. Nel 2005, la Libia ha dichiarato una zona di protezione della pesca, su un'area di mare estesa fino a 74 miglia dalla propria costa e dalla linea che chiude il Golfo della Sirte. In conseguenza di tale atto, le AutoritГ  libiche applicano, in maniera rigida, misure sanzionatorie, nei confronti delle imbarcazioni straniere impegnate in attivitГ  di pesca in detta area di mare, che si concretizzano, frequentemente, nellвҖҷintercettazione, sequestro e detenzione dei pescherecci stranieri e dei loro equipaggi, da parte delle AutoritГ  libiche e delle milizie locali. Sono state parimenti applicate consistenti sanzioni pecuniarie, oltre a provvedimenti di confisca delle imbarcazioni, delle attrezzature di pesca e dellвҖҷeventuale pescato. Nel 2017 la Libia ha inoltre dichiarato la propria area SAR (Search and Rescue) di ricerca e salvataggio dei migranti, adottando unвҖҷinterpretazione rigida della SAR e dissuadendo lвҖҷintervento altrui in quellвҖҷarea. Con il Decreto 727 del dicembre 2023, il Governo di UnitГ  Nazionale ha dichiarato, in aggiunta al mare territoriale, che si estende fino alle 12 miglia nautiche dalla linea di base, una zona contigua di 12 miglia nautiche, per un totale quindi di 24 miglia nautiche dalla linea di base. Patente: ГЁ necessaria la traduzione in arabo dei dati contenuti in quella italiana per periodi fino a tre mesi; per permanenze superiori ГЁ necessaria la patente internazionale. Si sottolinea, in ogni caso, che ogni spostamento nel Paese, su ruota, comporta un elevato rischio ed ГЁ fortemente sconsigliato, soprattutto se effettuato in autonomia. E' richiesto un Permesso di sicurezza da parte delle AutoritГ  locali per i cittadini italiani che si rechino a Bengasi oppure in altre localitГ  della Cirenaica. Ai fini del rilascio si consiglia di prendere contatto con uno sponsor locale.

themes.heading: Passaporto
themes.text: Obbligatorio, con validitГ  residua di almeno 6 (sei) mesi, al momento dellвҖҷarrivo nel Paese, recante una pagina intera libera. Non ГЁ ammesso alcun altro documento di viaggio. LвҖҷingresso in Libia ГЁ proibito a chi abbia sul passaporto il visto di Israele o altre prove di soggiorno o transito in Israele.

themes.heading: Visto di ingresso
themes.text: Obbligatorio. Si raccomanda di verificare - con l'Ambasciata di Libia a Roma o con i Consolati libici in Italia (Milano e Palermo) - le procedure di richiesta del visto e quelle di registrazione all'arrivo in Libia. Per i visti di affari, la persona fisica o giuridica deve avanzare richiesta presso lвҖҷUfficio Immigrazione libico di residenza. E' fortemente consigliato, anche per motivi di sicurezza, disporre dell'assistenza di un contatto locale, per facilitare le procedure all'arrivo all'Aeroporto di Tripoli Maitiga. Per poter far ingresso attraverso la frontiera orientale, ГЁ necessario un ulteriore permesso di sicurezza, rilasciato dall'AutoritГ  locale: ai fini del rilascio occorre acquisire la collaborazione di uno sponsor locale.

themes.heading: Viaggi all'estero dei minori
themes.text: Si prega di consultare lвҖҷApprofondimento di questo sito Documenti di viaggio- documenti per viaggi allвҖҷestero di minori.

themes.heading: Altre informazioni
themes.text: Si ricorda che i viaggi nel Paese sono sconsigliati, se non dettati da ragioni di necessitГ , lavoro o affari. I viaggi per turismo sono sconsigliati, anche qualora parte di pacchetti organizzati da Agenzie di Viaggio/Tour Operators. Alle Imprese o ai Liberi Professionisti italiani, che operassero nel Paese, si ribadisce di adottare ogni misura di sicurezza attiva e passiva, ritenuta idonea a tutelare il proprio personale, sulla base degli obblighi che discendono dalla vigente legislazione, in materia di sicurezza sui luoghi di lavoro.

themes.heading: FormalitГ  doganali e valutarie
themes.text: E' vietata lвҖҷimportazione di bevande alcoliche ed ГЁ sconsigliato importare salumi. EвҖҷ altresГ¬ vietato esportare dinari libici, in quantitГ  superiore a 200 LYD, che non sono - in ogni caso - convertibili allвҖҷestero. Per cambiare i dinari rimasti, all'uscita dal Paese, viene spesso richiesto di esibire le ricevute dei cambi effettuati durante il soggiorno.

themes.heading: Dati Paese
themes.text: Capitale: Tripoli Popolazione: 7,381 milioni (2024) Superficie: 1.759.540 km2, circa sei volte quella dell'Italia. Fuso orario: UTC +2; stesso orario, quando lвҖҷItalia adotta lвҖҷora legale. Lingue: arabo, poco diffuse, se non a livello elementare/scolastico, le lingue straniere. Tra queste, maggiormente conosciute, lвҖҷitaliano e l'inglese. Religioni: musulmana. Rispettato il culto delle altre religioni, in particolare quella cristiana (chiese cattoliche a Tripoli e Bengasi). Moneta: Dinaro Libico (DL). Prefisso telefonico dallвҖҷItalia: 00218 Collegamenti telefonici: I servizi di telefonia mobile in roaming sono solo parzialmente fruibili. ГҲ pertanto vivamente raccomandato, a coloro che giungano nel Paese, munirsi di schede telefoniche locali o utilizzare sistemi di comunicazione satellitare. Il servizio dati Internet, sulle reti di telefonia mobile, ГЁ disponibile. Clima: lungo la costa il clima dвҖҷestate ГЁ caldo, con temperature estive intorno ai 30/35 gradi, mentre gli inverni sono miti, con le precipitazioni concentrate tra novembre e gennaio. AllвҖҷinterno il clima ГЁ desertico, con picchi estivi fino a 48-50 gradi, mentre gli inverni possono essere molto rigidi, con temperature notturne intorno allo zero. In primavera, il Ghibli (vento sabbioso proveniente dal deserto) determina spesso un repentino innalzamento della temperatura sulla costa, anche di 10 gradi rispetto alla media e puГІ causare infiammazioni agli occhi e alle vie respiratorie.

themes.heading: Muut turvallisuustason tiedot
themes.text: UlkoministeriГ¶ kehottaa vГӨlttГӨmГӨГӨn kaikkea matkustamista Libyaan. Jos kuitenkin olet alueella, noudata ГӨГӨrimmГӨistГӨ varovaisuutta, seuraa paikallisviranomaisten ohjeita, tee matkustusilmoitus ja ole yhteydessГӨ omaisiisi. UM:n matkustustiedotteet ovat suosituksia Vastaat matkapГӨГӨtГ¶ksestГӨsi aina itse. Paikallisilla viranomaisilla on ensisijainen vastuu kaikkien maan alueella oleskelevien ihmisten turvallisuudesta ja hГӨtГӨtilanteissa etsintГӨ- ja pelastustoimista. Kriisitilanteissa noudata paikallisten viranomaisten ohjeita. Seuraa uutisointia tiedotusvГӨlineissГӨ sekГӨ tiedotusta edustuston verkkosivuilla ja sosiaalisen median kanavissa. UlkoministeriГ¶ ja Suomen ulkomaanedustustot avustavat suomalaisia konsulipalvelulain mukaisesti. MikГӨli Euroopan unionin ulkopuolisessa kohdemaassa ei ole Suomen edustustoa, voit kГӨГӨntyГӨ muiden EU-maiden edustustojen puoleen. Matkatoimiston jГӨrjestГӨmГӨllГӨ matkalla olevaa avustaa ensisijaisesti matkanjГӨrjestГӨjГӨ. Konsulipalvelulaki Konsulisuojeludirektiivi Laki matkapalveluyhdistelmistГӨ Suosittelemme aina matkavakuutuksen ottamista. Tarkista ennen matkaasi matkavakuutuksesi kattavuus ja voimassaolo erityisesti jos sinulla on jokin sairaus, olet raskaana, matkasi suuntautuu vaarallisille alueille tai harrastat riskialttiita lajeja, Sairastuneen tai vainajan kotiuttaminen on kallista ja vaikea jГӨrjestГӨГӨ ilman kattavaa matkavakuutusta, eikГӨ hoitoa aina saa ulkomailla ilman maksusitoumusta tai ennakkomaksua. Tee matkustusilmoitus osoitteessa https://matkustusilmoitus.fi/

themes.heading: Yleinen turvallisuustilanne
themes.text: Yleinen turvallisuustilanne on erittГӨin heikko ja arvaamaton. Turvallisuustilanne saattaa muuttua nopeasti. Konfliktin osapuolet ovat solmineet lokakuussa 2020 tulitauon. TГӨstГӨ huolimatta maassa on runsaasti aseistettuja ryhmiГӨ ja aseellisia yhteenottoja esiintyy eri puolilla maata. Tripolissa ainoa toiminnassa oleva kansainvГӨlinen lentokenttГӨ (Mitiga) on myГ¶s ollut aseellisten hyГ¶kkГӨysten kohteena. LentokenttiГӨ voidaan sulkea lyhyellГӨ vartoitusajalla ja lentoja perua. Terrori-iskut ovat myГ¶s mahdollisia. Seuraa paikallisviranomaisten ohjeita sekГӨ mediaa. VГӨltГӨ liikkumista pimeГӨn aikaan sekГӨ vГӨkijoukkoja. Varovaisuus ja valppaus ovat tarpeen koko maassa. Kidnappauksen uhka kohdistuu erityisesti lГӨnsimaalaisiin. VedestГӨ ja elintarvikkeista saattaa ajoittain olla pula. Internet- ja matkapuhelinyhteydet toimivat ajoittain heikosti. Koko maassa esiintyy toistuvia sГӨhkГ¶katkoksia. Suomen kunniakonsulaatti on ollut vГӨliaikaisesti suljettuna 31.12.2015 lГӨhtien. Maassa on tГӨllГӨ hetkellГӨ edustettuna muutama EU-maa, joiden edustustojen/konsulaattien yhteystiedot lГ¶ytyvГӨt Euroopan komission verkkosivuilta.

themes.heading: Erityiset turvallisuusriskit
themes.text: Kidnappausriski erityisesti ulkomaalaisia kohtaan on olemassa.

themes.heading: Rikollisuus
themes.text: Jos passisi katoaa tai varastetaan, tee siitГӨ ilmoitus paikalliselle poliisiasemalle. MikГӨli kohdemaassa ei ole Suomen edustustoa, hae hГӨtГӨmatkustusasiakirjaa (ETD = Emergency Travel Document) toisen EU-maan edustustosta. Ota kopiot tГӨrkeimmistГӨ asiapapereista kuten passista ja lentolipuista. SГӨilytГӨ ne erillГӨГӨn alkuperГӨisistГӨ.

themes.heading: Terveys
themes.text: Terveyspalvelujen taso on heikko. PГӨtevГӨstГӨ hoitohenkilГ¶kunnasta on pulaa kaikkialla samoin kuin lГӨГӨkkeistГӨ ja muista sairaalatarvikkeista. SelvitГӨ ennakkoon matkakohteesi tartuntatautitilanne ja rokotussuositukset. LГ¶ydГӨt ne seuraavien viranomaisten verkkosivuilta: Matkailijan terveysopas Maailman terveysjГӨrjestГ¶ WHO Euroopan tartuntatautivirasto

themes.heading: Maan lait ja kГӨytГӨnnГ¶t
themes.text: Valokuvaamiseen ja videokuvaamiseen liittyy monissa maissa rajoituksia. Mahdollisia kuvauskieltoja on syytГӨ noudattaa. YleensГӨ esim. sotilaallisten laitosten, lentokenttien tai satamien valokuvaaminen ja videokuvaaminen on kielletty, ja kielto koskee myГ¶s ilmakuvausta drooneilla. MyГ¶s droonien maahantuonti voi vaatia erillisiГӨ lupaprosesseja. Varmista tarvittaessa rajoitukset etukГӨteen paikallisviranomaisilta. KiellettyjГӨ kohteita kuvaava henkilГ¶ saatetaan ottaa kiinni tai pidГӨttГӨГӨ, ja kuvauslaitteet saatetaan tuhota tai takavarikoida.

themes.heading: MaahantulosГӨГӨnnГ¶kset
themes.text: UM ei vastaa vieraiden valtioiden maahantulosГӨГӨnnГ¶ksistГӨ, kuten passin voimassaoloa koskevista ehdoista tai viisumivelvollisuudesta. Varmista ajantasaiset maahantulosГӨГӨdГ¶kset kohdemaan viranomaisilta, esimerkiksi maan lГӨhimmГӨstГӨ edustustosta. Lue lisГӨГӨ Suomen kansalaisen matkustusasiakirjoista osoitteessa https://um.fi/passi/

Das KonsulargebГјhrengesetz ermГӨchtigt die Republik Г–sterreich, die Kosten fГјr allenfalls erforderliche SchutzmaГҹnahmen bzw. For ambulance, fire or police phone: 1415 Depending what you need, contact your: family and friends travel agent insurance provider employer airline Latest update We continue to advise do not travel to Libya due to the dangerous security situation and the high threat of terrorism and kidnapping. If you're in Libya, you should leave as soon as possible if it's safe to do so. Australia doesn't have a diplomatic mission in Libya. Our ability to provide consular assistance to Australians in Libya is severely limited (see 'Safety'). Advice Level summary We advise: Do not travel to Libya due to the dangerous security situation and the high threat of terrorism and kidnapping.

themes.heading: Safety
themes.text: Do not travel to Libya. Armed conflict between groups continues to occur in all parts of the country. The security situation is very unstable. There's a high threat of terrorist attack, kidnapping and crime. Australia doesn't have a diplomatic mission in Libya. If you're in Libya, leave as soon as possible if it's safe to do so. Ongoing instability limits our ability to provide consular assistance to Australians. If you choose to stay in Libya, seek professional security advice. Monitor the media for updated information about the security situation. Areas of Libya that border Algeria, Niger, Chad and Sudan are very dangerous. Bandits and armed groups operate in these areas. There's also a very high threat of kidnapping. If you are in a conflict zone, shelter in place until it is safe to leave, carry emergency supplies including food and water, and ensure your travel documents are valid and kept secure. It's illegal to drink alcohol in Libya. If you decide to consume alcohol despite our advice, you should be alert to the potential risks around drink spiking and methanol poisoning from consuming alcoholic drinks. Libya experiences severe weather. If you travel to Libya, follow regional weather forecasts and plan accordingly. Full travel advice: Safety

themes.heading: Health
themes.text: Waterborne, foodborne, parasitic and other infectious diseases are common. They include Leishmaniasis, West Nile virus, typhoid, hepatitis, tuberculosis, plague and measles. It's not safe to drink tap water or to swim in freshwater. Only drink bottled water with sealed lids or boiled water. Medical facilities are limited. Some medicines are in short supply, particularly for chronic conditions. Carry some medical supplies for personal use. If you're seriously ill or injured, you may need to be evacuated. Make sure your insurance covers this. Medical evacuations can be expensive and hard to organise. Full travel advice: Health

themes.heading: Local laws
themes.text: The rule of law is not established in Libya. Police services may not operate in all areas. Penalties for drug offences are severe. They include the death penalty or life imprisonment. This also applies to other serious offences like murder. Libya may not recognise dual citizenship. Women without a male guardian may be restricted in their ability to travel. Officials may seize your passport if you're involved in a business dispute. They may not let you leave Libya until the dispute is settled. Many activities are illegal in Libya. This includes same-sex relationships and drinking alcohol. You could be jailed for having sex outside marriage. Islamic law decides child custody. A child born to a Libyan father is a Libyan citizen. They need their father's permission to leave Libya. Full travel advice: Local laws

themes.heading: Travel
themes.text: A visa is required to enter Libya. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Contact the nearest embassy or consulate of Libya for the latest details. We can't advise you on the safety of departure options. If you plan to leave, you need to determine the safest route out of the country. In areas where conflict occurs, the best course of action may be to stay where you are until it becomes safer to leave. Airports and land border crossings can close without warning. Plan for delays and temporary closures. Trying to leave Libya via land borders is very dangerous. There's a high threat of kidnapping in regions bordering Algeria, Chad, Sudan and Niger. Don't try to leave Libya through these countries or through illegal border crossings. Officials may detain you if you can't prove you entered Libya at an official border crossing. You must always carry ID documents. Upon arrival, you need to register your passport with a police station. If you don't, you could be fined. Full travel advice: Travel

themes.heading: Local contacts
themes.text: There are limits to what consular assistance we can provide in high-risk locations. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. If you choose to travel or stay in Libya despite our advice, our ability to provide consular services in Libya is extremely limited. Australia doesn't have a diplomatic mission in Libya. For consular advice, contact the Australian Embassy in Rome, Italy. Full travel advice: Local contacts

themes.heading: Armed conflict
themes.text: Armed conflict is occurring across the country, including in Tripoli. Fighting can break out anywhere without warning. If you're in Libya, your life and safety could be at risk. Some foreigners have been killed. Militias have targeted ports, airports, and oil and gas installations. If you travel to Libya despite our advice: leave Libya as soon as it's safe to do so monitor local and international media for possible risks seek professional security advice arrange personal security measures

themes.heading: Consular services
themes.text: Australia doesn't have a diplomatic mission in Libya. Only a few diplomatic missions and international bodies are in Libya. Our ability to provide consular services in Libya is severely limited. For consular advice, contact the Australian Embassy in Rome, Italy.

themes.heading: Terrorism
themes.text: Terrorism is a threat worldwide. Terrorist attacks could occur anywhere and at any time. Be alert to possible threats, especially in public places. Terrorism remains a serious threat in Libya, particularly in Fezzan and south of Sirte. Terrorist groups engage in ongoing clashes. Terrorists often use small arms, rocket-propelled grenades or improvised bombs. They target Libyan authorities and security interests. Attacks on foreign interests have also occurred, including: hotels and restaurants locations visited by foreigners, including shopping markets public transportation systems remaining diplomatic interests international non-government organisations To stay safe: check the media for new threats take official warnings seriously If thereвҖҷs a terrorist incident, leave the area if itвҖҷs safe to do so and follow the instructions of local authorities. More information: Terrorism

themes.heading: Kidnapping
themes.text: Kidnapping occurs across the world with political, ideological and criminal motives. Foreigners, including Australians, have been kidnapped overseas whilst travelling. Kidnaps can happen anywhere, even in places that seem safe. Kidnapping occurs in all regions of Libya, and foreign nationals are frequently targeted. A variety of criminal groups use kidnapping for financial or political gain, particularly in urban areas along LibyaвҖҷs coastline. Militia checkpoints throughout the countryвҖҷs interior also act as prime locations for potential kidnapping. If despite our advice you travel to areas with a high risk of kidnapping, our ability to provide consular assistance in these destinations is extremely limited. YouвҖҷre responsible for your own safety. You can find more information on how to reduce your risk of kidnapping here: Kidnapping The Australian Government's longstanding policy is that it doesn't make payments or concessions to kidnappers. Ransom payments to kidnappers have funded further terrorist attacks and criminal activity. Ransom payments to terrorist groups are likely to contravene Australian counter-terrorism financing laws. More information: Kidnapping Scams that affect travellers

themes.heading: Civil unrest and political tension
themes.text: Libya's political situation remains volatile. The rule of law is not established in Libya. Outbreaks of violence can occur with little warning, including in Tripoli. Public protests and events that draw large groups of people can turn violent. Protesters have previously set fire to government buildings. If you travel to Libya despite our advice: avoid protests and demonstrations remain vigilant monitor the media and other sources for possible unrest or strikes More information: Protests and civil unrest

themes.heading: Violent crime
themes.text: Crime levels in Libya are high. Law and order has broken down in most of the country. Violent crime is widespread. Foreigners are at high risk of violent crime. Crimes include carjackings targeting foreign-owned vehicles, extortion and robbery by armed groups.

themes.heading: Border-area violence
themes.text: Areas of Libya that border with Algeria, Niger, Chad and Sudan are very dangerous. Avoid cross-border travel between Libya and these countries. Bandits and armed groups operate in these areas. There's also a very high threat of kidnapping. Violence and armed robberies have been reported as vehicles leave Libya by road. This includes roads that cross into Tunisia.

themes.heading: Extreme weather
themes.text: Libya experiences extreme weather. Libya's rainy season is from November to March. Temperatures can reach 40В°C between June and September. If, despite our advice, you decide to travel to or stay in Libya, follow regional weather forecasts and plan accordingly. Register with the Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System to receive alerts on major disasters.

themes.heading: Travel Insurance
themes.text: As Libya is a вҖҳdo not travelвҖҷ location, standard travel insurance is unlikely to provide coverage. You should ensure your travel insurance specifically covers high-risk destinations including Libya. You need special travel insurance if youвҖҷre going to a high-risk destination, which can be expensive. We canвҖҷt guarantee your safety and security in another country or pay for your medical expenses, including evacuation. If you donвҖҷt have insurance, you shouldnвҖҷt travel.

themes.heading: Physical and mental health
themes.text: Travelling overseas can be stressful and uncertain. You may experience unfamiliar environments, travel disruptions, changes in normal routines and limited access to health care. Not taking your prescribed medications is a common cause of mental ill-health for Australians overseas. If you are concerned about your welfare, or someone else's, contact your nearest Australian embassy, high commission, or consulate. You can also call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on +61 2 6261 3305. Mental health resources: Information and advice on mental illness (HealthDirect). Living with mental illness (BeyondBlue) Medicare Mental Health (the Department of Health and Aged Care) More information: General health advice Mental health support overseas

themes.heading: Infectious diseases
themes.text: Waterborne, foodborne, parasitic and other infectious diseases are common. These include: typhoid hepatitis tuberculosis plague measles leishmaniasis West Nile virus Serious outbreaks sometimes occur. To protect yourself from illness: drink boiled water or bottled water with sealed lids don't swim in fresh water take precautions against flea bites don't handle dead animals If you suspect food poisoning, seek urgent medical attention. Also seek medical attention if you have a fever or diarrhoea. More information: Infectious diseases

themes.heading: Medical care
themes.text: You'll need to be evacuated if you get seriously ill or injured. Medical evacuation may not be possible and can be very expensive. Most Australian travel insurance policies won't cover you for travel to Libya. Medical facilities in Libya are limited. Some medications are in short supply. Medical facilities in remote areas can be basic or non-existent. Carry some medical supplies for personal use.

themes.heading: LGBTQIA+ travellers
themes.text: Libyan law prohibits same-sex relationships. Same-sex marriage is also illegal. Convicted offenders face prison sentences. More information: Advice for LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Drugs
themes.text: Penalties for drug offences are severe. They include the death penalty or life in jail. More information: Carrying or using drugs

themes.heading: Local customs
themes.text: The Islamic holy month of Ramadan is observed in Libya. Respect religious and cultural customs and laws at this time. During Ramadan, eating, drinking and smoking may be illegal in public during the day. If you're not fasting, avoid these activities around people who are. Seek local advice to avoid offence.

themes.heading: Visas and border measures
themes.text: Each country or territory decides its own entry and exit rules, including health border screening measures. To find out what you need to enter a destination, ask their embassy, consulate or immigration office.

themes.heading: Visa conditions
themes.text: If, despite our advice, you decide to travel to Libya, you need a visa. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Contact the nearest embassy or consulate for details about visas, currency, customs and other travel requirements.

themes.heading: Border measures
themes.text: Officials may detain you if you can't prove you entered at an official border crossing. We cannot offer advice on the safety of individual departure options, including any flights that become available, or on travel to a departure point. You should make your own judgement about the safest route out of the country, taking account of the advice of local authorities.

themes.heading: Passport registration
themes.text: If you travel to Libya despite our advice, you must register your passport with a police station on arrival. If it's safe to do so, go to the police station closest to the place you're staying. Failure to do so may result in fines and problems during your stay or on departure.

themes.heading: Yellow fever vaccination
themes.text: You need a valid yellow fever vaccination certificate to enter Libya if you arrive from a country with a risk of yellow fever. Some airlines may want to see one when you leave. More information: Countries with a risk of yellow fever

themes.heading: Travel via Israel
themes.text: If your passport shows entry to Israel, you won't be able to enter Libya.

themes.heading: Lost or stolen passport
themes.text: Your passport is valuable and can be used by others to commit crimes. DonвҖҷt let anyone trick you into giving it to them. Always keep it in a safe place. If your passport is lost or stolen, tell the Australian Government: In Australia: contact the Australian Passport Information Service. Overseas: contact the nearest Australian embassy or consulate.

themes.heading: Passport with вҖҳXвҖҷ gender identifier
themes.text: Australian passports comply with international standards for sex and gender. If your passport has an 'X' in the sex field, we canвҖҷt guarantee your entry or transit to other countries. Ask the embassy or consulate of your destinations if theyвҖҷll accept your passport with an 'X' gender marker. Check all transit destinations too. More information: LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Money
themes.text: The local currency is the Libyan Dinar (LYD). Libya law prohibits importing or exporting the Libyan Dinar. Libya is a cash-based economy. Debit cards, credit cards and travellerвҖҷs cheques are not often accepted, and ATMs are rare. Official exchange offices will not accept Australian dollars. Withdrawing money from banks may be difficult due to severe shortages of cash. The security situation sometimes disrupts banking services.

themes.heading: Landmines
themes.text: Unexploded remnants of war, including landmines, are a hazard throughout Libya.

themes.heading: Road travel
themes.text: Road travel within Libya is dangerous. There's a risk of carjacking and robbery as well as being caught up in outbreaks of localised violence. Don't attempt to leave Libya via borders with Algeria, Chad, Sudan, or Niger. There's a very high threat of kidnapping. All border crossings can close without warning. Plan for delays and temporary road closures. The road between Tripoli and Zawiya is vulnerable to attack. Convoys, including diplomatic convoys, have been attacked. If you plan to cross the border to Tunisia at the Ras Jedir checkpoint, plan your journey carefully. Check the border crossing is open before you leave. Be prepared for armed clashes at or on the way to the border. Road traffic accidents are common and the emergency response to a road traffic accident may be limited. Road conditions vary across the country. Driving standards are poor and traffic is heavy. In the desert, roads are unpaved and wind-blown sand can reduce visibility quickly. There may be fuel shortages and long queues at fuel stations.

themes.heading: Air travel
themes.text: DFAT doesnвҖҷt give advice about how safe different airlines or flight paths are. Safety risks and weather events may cause flight delays and cancellations. Contact your travel agent or airline to confirm your plans. Airlines and airports can restrict what you can take in your luggage. Regulations may be different to those in Australia. Check with your airline before you travel. Air travel and airport operations are subject to disruption. Check with your travel provider about route options and airport operations at your planned point of arrival or departure. Check Libya's air safety profile with the Aviation Safety Network. More information: Air travel

themes.heading: Emergencies
themes.text: Depending on what you need, contact your: family and friends travel agent insurance provider employer airline Emergency services exist in Libya, but you shouldn't expect them to help you. For ambulance, fire or police phone: 1415 The rule of law has not been re-established in Libya. Police and medical services may not be operating in all parts of the country.

themes.heading: Consular contacts
themes.text: There are limits to what consular assistance we can provide in high-risk locations. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. Ongoing unrest limits our consular help to Australians who are in Libya. We don't have an Australian diplomatic mission in Libya. For consular advice, contact the Australian Embassy in Rome, Italy.

themes.heading: Australian Embassy, Rome
themes.text: Via Antonio Bosio 5 00161 Rome, Italy Phone: +39 06 85 2721 Email: consular-rome@dfat.gov.au Website: Australia in Italy Facebook: Australian Embassy, Italy X: @AusAmbItaly

themes.heading: 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre
themes.text: In a consular emergency, if you can't contact an embassy, call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on: +61 2 6261 3305 from overseas 1300 555 135 in Australia

themes.heading: Country summary
themes.text: Reissued after periodic review with minor edits. Do not travel to Libya for any reason due to crime, terrorism, unexploded landmines, civil unrest, kidnapping, and armed conflict. Read entire Travel Advisory. Country Summary: Terrorism: There is risk of terrorist violence, including terrorist attacks and other activity in Libya. Visit the U.S. Department of State's country reports on terrorism to learn more. Terrorist attacks could occur with little or no warning, targeting public spaces like tourist locations, hotels, transportation hubs, markets/shopping malls, and government facilities. Kidnapping is widespread, and victims have included U.S. citizens. Kidnappers may take advantage of unplanned opportunities. Criminal or armed individuals or groups have threatened to and/or have seized or detained and threatened to kill, injure, or continue to detain individuals in order to compel a third party (including a governmental organization) to do or abstain from doing something as a condition of release. Unexploded landmines, cluster munitions, and unexploded ammunition are a hazard throughout Libya. Heed land mine warning signs. Do not venture off the road into areas marked with red and white plastic tape. Avoid roadside ditches, shoulders, and unmarked trails. Never touch anything resembling unexploded munitions. Landmines do exist in populated areas due to numerous ground offensives in the last decade. Outbreaks of violence between competing armed groups can occur with little warning and have the potential to impact U.S. citizens. The capital, Tripoli, and other cities, such as Surman, Al-Jufra, Misrata, Ajdabiya, Benghazi, and Zuwara, have witnessed fighting among armed groups. Hotels and airports frequented by U.S. citizens have been the targets of these attacks. Even demonstrations intended to be peaceful can turn confrontational and escalate into violence. Armed groups sometimes detain travelers for arbitrary reasons, do not grant detainees access to lawyers or legal processes, and do not allow detainees to inform others of their status. U.S. citizens should carry proof of citizenship and valid immigration status at all times, but having these documents does not guarantee fair treatment. While there are operational international airports in Libya with regular domestic and international flights, flights are often delayed, rerouted, and cancelled without warning. The U.S. government prohibits U.S. commercial aviation operations within Libyan airspace. Due to risks to civil aviation operating within or in the vicinity of Libya, the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has issued a Notice to Air Missions (NOTAM) and a Special Federal Aviation Regulation (SFAR). For more information U.S. citizens should consult the Federal Aviation AdministrationвҖҷs Prohibitions, Restrictions and Notices. The Commandant of the U.S. Coast Guard has determined that effective anti-terrorism measures are not in place in Libyan ports and has imposed conditions of entry on vessels that arrive in U.S. ports having visited ports in Libya. Mariners and passengers traveling through the ports of Libya should exercise caution. U.S. citizens in Libya in need of routine consular services should contact the U.S. Embassy in Tunis, Tunisia for assistance. To contact the U.S. Embassy in Tunis, click here. U.S. citizens who are in Libya are urged to depart as soon as possible via commercial means if possible. The Department of State will continue to provide information via the Smart Traveler Enrollment Program (STEP), Embassy Libya's web page, Travel.State.Gov, Facebook, and X. Read the country information page for additional information on travel to Libya. If you decide to travel to Libya: Visit our website for Travel to High-Risk Areas. Carry proof of citizenship and valid immigration status at all times. Avoid demonstrations and crowds. Make contingency plans to leave. Draft a will and designate appropriate insurance beneficiaries and a power of attorney. Discuss a plan with loved ones regarding care or custody of children, pets, property, belongings, non-liquid assets (collections, artwork, etc.), funeral wishes, etcetera. Establish your own personal security plan in coordination with your family, and employer or host organization, so that they can monitor your safety and location. This plan should specify whom you would contact first, and how that person should share information. Appoint one family member to serve as the point of contact with kidnappers/hostage-takers, media, U.S. and host country government agencies, and members of Congress if you are kidnapped, taken hostage, or detained. Consider consulting with a professional security organization. Do not touch unknown metal objects and avoid traveling off well-used roads, tracks, and paths due to risk of unexploded ordnance. Enroll in the Smart Traveler Enrollment Program (STEP) to receive Alerts and make it easier to locate you in an emergency. Review the Country Security Report for Libya. Visit the CDC page for the latest Travel Health Information related to your travel. U.S. citizens who travel abroad should always have a contingency plan for emergency situations. Review the TravelerвҖҷs Checklist. We highly recommend that you buy insurance before you travel. Check with your travel insurance provider about evacuation assistance, medical insurance, and trip cancellation coverage.
